1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus
7	
8	/-! # The Jacobian of a smooth proper curve
9	
10	The Jacobian of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field, equipped with
11	its structure as an abelian variety.
12	
13	## Status (Phase C scaffolding)
14	
15	The Jacobian is defined uniformly as the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness
16	(`JacobianWitness`) for the curve `C`. Existence of such a witness is the single
17	remaining mathematical sorry of the Phase-C scaffolding (`nonempty_jacobianWitness`);
18	the four protected instances on `Jacobian C` all project from the witness directly.
19	
20	The file contains:
21	- `IsAlbanese`: the Albanese universal property for a pointed curve.
22	- `IsAlbanese.unique`: uniqueness of the Albanese object up to canonical isomorphism.
23	- `JacobianWitness`: a bundled candidate Albanese object together with the universal
24	  property uniformly over the choice of $k$-rational marked point.
25	- `nonempty_jacobianWitness`: the (deferred) existence of such a witness — the single
26	  Phase-C sorry, absorbing both higher-genus Albanese existence and genus-`0` rigidity.
27	- `Jacobian` and its four protected instances, each obtained by projection from the
28	  Albanese witness.
29	
30	### Forbidden shortcut (sanity check)
31	
32	Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))` *unconditionally* (the terminal object,
33	i.e. `Spec k`) compiles and discharges three of the four instances for free, but the
34	fourth instance `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (𝟙_ _).hom` would force `genus C = 0`,
35	which is mathematically wrong for any curve of genus `≥ 1`. The terminal-object definition
36	is therefore forbidden when `genus C > 0`. The witness-based definition avoids this
37	issue: in genus `0` the witness's underlying scheme `J` is `Spec k`, and in genus `> 0`
38	the witness's `J` is the honest Albanese object.
39	-/
40	
41	set_option autoImplicit false
42	
43	universe u
44	
45	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
46	
47	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
48	
49	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
50	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
51	  [IsProper C.hom]
52	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
53	
54	/-- The Albanese universal property for a pointed smooth proper curve `(C, P)`.
55	An object `J` (with abelian-variety structure) is the Albanese of `(C, P)` if it
56	receives a universal pointed morphism `C ⟶ J` sending `P` to the identity. -/
57	def IsAlbanese (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
58	    (J : Over (Spec (.of k)))
59	    [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] : Prop :=
60	  ∃ α : C ⟶ J, P ≫ α = η[J] ∧
61	    ∀ {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom]
62	      [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (_ : P ≫ f = η[A]),
63	      ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = α ≫ g
64	
65	namespace IsAlbanese
66	
67	noncomputable def ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
68	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
69	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) : C ⟶ J :=
70	  Classical.choose h
71	
72	lemma comp_ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
73	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
74	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) :
75	    P ≫ h.ofCurve = η[J] :=
76	  (Classical.choose_spec h).1
77	
78	lemma exists_unique_ofCurve_comp {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
79	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
80	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J)
81	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
82	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
83	    ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = h.ofCurve ≫ g :=
84	  (Classical.choose_spec h).2 f hf
85	
86	/-- The Albanese object is unique up to a unique isomorphism compatible with the
87	universal morphisms. -/
88	theorem unique {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))} {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C}
89	    {J₁ J₂ : Over (Spec (.of k))}
90	    [GrpObj J₁] [IsProper J₁.hom] [Smooth J₁.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₁.hom]
91	    [GrpObj J₂] [IsProper J₂.hom] [Smooth J₂.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₂.hom]
92	    (h₁ : IsAlbanese C P J₁) (h₂ : IsAlbanese C P J₂) :
93	    ∃! (e : J₁ ⟶ J₂), h₂.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ e := by
94	  have ⟨g, hg_eq, hg_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
95	  have ⟨h, hh_eq, hh_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
96	  have ⟨g₁, hg₁_eq, hg₁_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
97	  have g₁_eq_id : g₁ = 𝟙 J₁ := by
98	    refine (hg₁_unique (𝟙 J₁) ?_).symm
99	    simp
100	  have ggh_eq_g₁ : g ≫ h = g₁ := by
101	    refine hg₁_unique (g ≫ h) ?_
102	    change h₁.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ (g ≫ h)
103	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hg_eq, ← hh_eq]
104	  have hgh : g ≫ h = 𝟙 J₁ := by rw [ggh_eq_g₁, g₁_eq_id]
105	  have ⟨k₂, hk₂_eq, hk₂_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
106	  have k₂_eq_id : k₂ = 𝟙 J₂ := by
107	    refine (hk₂_unique (𝟙 J₂) ?_).symm
108	    simp
109	  have hhg_eq_k₂ : h ≫ g = k₂ := by
110	    refine hk₂_unique (h ≫ g) ?_
111	    change h₂.ofCurve = h₂.ofCurve ≫ (h ≫ g)
112	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hh_eq, ← hg_eq]
113	  have hhg : h ≫ g = 𝟙 J₂ := by rw [hhg_eq_k₂, k₂_eq_id]
114	  exact ⟨g, hg_eq, fun g' hg' => hg_unique g' hg'⟩
115	
116	end IsAlbanese
117	
118	/-- The identity morphism on `Spec k` is geometrically irreducible.
119	This is a small helper needed for the genus-0 case of `Jacobian`. -/
120	lemma geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec (k : Type u) [Field k] :
121	    GeometricallyIrreducible (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))) where
122	  geometrically_irreducibleSpace := by
123	    intro K _ y Z fst snd h
124	    haveI : IsIso snd := h.isIso_snd_of_isIso
125	    exact ObjectProperty.prop_of_iso (P := (IrreducibleSpace · : ObjectProperty Scheme))
126	      (asIso snd).symm inferInstance
127	
128	/-- A bundled Albanese witness for the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
129	`C`: a candidate group scheme `J` (with proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible
130	structure, smooth of relative dimension `genus C`), and a proof that `J` satisfies
131	the Albanese universal property of Definition `IsAlbanese` for `C` *for every choice
132	of $k$-rational marked point* `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`.
133	
134	Mathematically, the underlying scheme `J` (and its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
135	structure) is intrinsic to `C`; only the universal morphism `C ⟶ J` depends on the
136	choice of marked point `P`. Encoding `isAlbaneseFor` as a `∀ P, IsAlbanese …` field
137	keeps the bundle independent of any specific pointing, which is what
138	`AbelJacobi.Jacobian.ofCurve P` needs in order to project the Albanese morphism for
139	an *arbitrary* input `P`.
140	
141	The smoothness witness is recorded at the specific relative dimension `genus C`,
142	which is the form required by `Jacobian.smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`. -/
143	structure JacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
144	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] where
145	  /-- The candidate Albanese object. -/
146	  J : Over (Spec (.of k))
147	  /-- The group-scheme structure on `J`. -/
148	  grpObj : GrpObj J
149	  /-- `J` is proper over `k`. -/
150	  proper : IsProper J.hom
151	  /-- `J` is smooth over `k`. -/
152	  smooth : Smooth J.hom
153	  /-- `J` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
154	  geomIrred : GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom
155	  /-- `J` is smooth of relative dimension `genus C` (so dim `J = genus C`). -/
156	  smoothGenus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) J.hom
157	  /-- The Albanese universal-property data: for every choice of $k$-rational marked
158	  point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`, the scheme `J` together with its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
159	  structure satisfies the Albanese universal property for the pointed curve `(C, P)`. -/
160	  isAlbaneseFor : ∀ (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C), @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred
161	
162	/-- Existence of an Albanese witness for every smooth proper geometrically irreducible
163	curve. This is the single remaining mathematical sorry of the Phase-C Jacobian
164	scaffolding: it packages all five protected sorries (`Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`,
165	`smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`, `instGeometricallyIrreducible`)
166	into a single existence hypothesis, *and* records that the Albanese property of the
167	underlying scheme `J` is uniform over every choice of $k$-rational marked point
168	`P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C` (via the `isAlbaneseFor` field of `JacobianWitness`).
169	
170	Mathematically this corresponds to the construction of the Albanese variety of `C`,
171	classically via symmetric powers $\mathrm{Sym}^g(C)$ and the Abel--Jacobi map
172	(Brill--Noether--Riemann--Roch), or equivalently as the identity component of the
173	Picard scheme. Both routes require infrastructure not yet available in Mathlib
174	(quotients of schemes by finite group actions; FGA representability), so the
175	existence is assumed and the proof is deferred to a future iteration. -/
176	theorem nonempty_jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
177	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
178	    Nonempty (JacobianWitness C) :=
179	  sorry
180	
181	/-- A choice of Albanese witness for `C`, extracted via `Classical.choice`.
182	Used to define `Jacobian C` and to discharge each of the four protected instances
183	on it. -/
184	noncomputable def jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
185	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
186	    JacobianWitness C :=
187	  Classical.choice (nonempty_jacobianWitness C)
188	
189	-- data
190	/-- The Jacobian of a smooth, proper curve over a field `k`.
191	
192	The Jacobian is the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness for `C` (see
193	`JacobianWitness` and `nonempty_jacobianWitness`); the existence of such a
194	witness is the single remaining mathematical sorry of the Phase-C scaffolding.
195	The genus-`0` specialisation is implicit in the witness — a smooth proper
196	geometrically irreducible group scheme over `k` of relative dimension `0` is
197	`Spec k` — and the genus-`0` rigidity content is absorbed into
198	`nonempty_jacobianWitness`. -/
199	noncomputable def Jacobian (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
200	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
201	  (jacobianWitness C).J
202	
203	namespace Jacobian
204	
205	/-! ## The Jacobian of `C` is an abelian variety. -/
206	
207	-- data
208	/-- The group scheme structure on the Jacobian of the curve `C`. -/
209	noncomputable instance instGrpObj : GrpObj (Jacobian C) := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
210	
211	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is smooth of relative dimension `g` over `k`, where `g` is the
212	genus of `C`. -/
213	instance smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (Jacobian C).hom :=
214	  (jacobianWitness C).smoothGenus
215	
216	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is proper over `k`. -/
217	instance instIsProper : IsProper (Jacobian C).hom := (jacobianWitness C).proper
218	
219	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
220	instance instGeometricallyIrreducible : GeometricallyIrreducible (Jacobian C).hom :=
221	  (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
222	
223	end Jacobian
224	
225	end AlgebraicGeometry

1	---
2	name: lean-auditor
3	description: Whole-project read-only audit of all .lean code, with no strategy bias. Per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, bad Lean practices. Critical pushback on excuse-comments ("temporary wrong def", "will fix later") — treats them as red flags, not workflow.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: true
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - Dispatch me every review phase. I am mandatory.
11	  - Pass me as LITTLE strategic context as possible. Do NOT include
12	    STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, or "this is what we are trying to prove"
13	    framing in the directive. My value depends on auditing the Lean
14	    *without* bias toward what the strategy claims should be true.
15	  - Acceptable directive content: a list of files, optional focus areas
16	    (e.g. "pay extra attention to Algebra/"), and the absolute paths
17	    to read. That's it.
18	  - My output is a per-file checklist plus a flagged-issues block. Use
19	    it to seed `recommendations.md` and (when severe) trigger a
20	    refactor-subagent directive next iter.

22	    Lean; the blueprint-checker audits Lean against blueprint. Run
23	    both for full coverage.
24	---
25	
26	# Lean Auditor
27	
28	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
29	
30	You are **mandatory in the review phase**: the review agent dispatches you every iteration. Your output flags issues the prover and plan agents may have missed.
31	
32	## Stance: no strategic bias
33	
34	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
35	
36	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
37	
38	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
39	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
40	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
41	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
42	
43	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
44	
45	## Directive Format
46	
47	```markdown
48	# Lean Auditor Directive
49	
50	## Slug
51	<slug>
52	
53	## Scope (files)
54	<"all" by default. Optionally a list of file paths if the review agent wants to narrow scope; never expand beyond the project.>
55	
56	## Focus areas (optional)
57	<directories or namespaces to pay extra attention to. Bias toward thoroughness — do not skip the rest of the project just because focus is named.>
58	
59	## Known issues
60	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
61	```
62	
63	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.
64	
65	## What you do
66	
67	1. Read your directive.
68	2. **List every `.lean` file** under the project. Use `find` or `ls -R` — do not rely on import graphs (some files may be temporarily orphaned).
69	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
70	   - Read the whole file.
71	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
72	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
73	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
74	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
75	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.
76	
77	You may use:
78	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics. Useful to verify a declaration's actual type.
79	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to get sorry counts per file.
80	- Standard `Read` / `Grep` for code reading.
81	
82	You may NOT modify any project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
83	
84	## Report format
85	
86	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-auditor-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
87	
88	```markdown
89	# Lean Audit Report
90	
91	## Slug
92	<slug>
93	
94	## Iteration
95	<NNN>
96	
97	## Scope
98	- files audited: <N>
99	- files skipped (per directive): <N> — <reason>
100	
101	## Per-file checklist
102	
103	### <path/to/file.lean>
104	- **outdated comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
105	- **suspect definitions**: <N flagged> (or "none")
106	- **dead-end proofs**: <N flagged> (or "none")
107	- **bad practices**: <N flagged> (or "none")
108	- **excuse-comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
109	- **notes**:
110	  - <one bullet per finding, naming line number where relevant>
111	  - ...
112	
113	### <next file>
114	- ...
115	
116	(One block per file. Cover every file, including the ones with no findings — they get `none` everywhere and `notes: -`.)
117	
118	## Must-fix-this-iter
119	
120	Apply verbatim. Every one of the following lands here automatically, no exceptions, no under-classification:
121	
122	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
123	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
124	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.
125	- **Suspect bodies on substantive claims**: `:= sorry` on a load-bearing claim, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial statement, `:= Classical.choice _` without authorization.
126	- **Axioms** on non-trivial claims that the project hasn't explicitly authorized in the strategy.
127	
128	A finding that meets any of the above lands at must-fix-this-iter — even if classifying it as "major" would feel more diplomatic. Soft severity is how wrong code hardens into the project.
129	
130	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>. Why must-fix: <one line>.
131	- ...
132	
133	## Major
134	
135	Findings that are real issues but don't meet the must-fix bar — typically stale comments that don't actively mislead, naming drift, minor code smells.
136	
137	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
138	- ...
139	
140	## Minor
141	
142	Low-impact observations.
143	
144	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
145	- ...
146	
147	## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)
148	
149	If any excuse-comments were flagged, list each one verbatim with its file:line. These deserve special visibility because they document the project lying to itself.
150	
151	- `Foo/Bar.lean:42`: "TODO: replace with the real definition" (attached to `Foo.bar`, which is a load-bearing definition in `Baz.lean`). Severity: critical.
152	- ...
153	
154	## Severity summary
155	
156	- **must-fix-this-iter**: <N> — these block downstream work in their files until addressed (see plan.md's per-file gate).
157	- **major**: <N>
158	- **minor**: <N>
159	- **excuse-comments**: <N> (also counted under must-fix-this-iter above; called out separately because they document the project lying to itself).
160	
161	Overall verdict: one sentence.
162	```
163	
164	**Strict severity reminder.** The plan agent's gate treats must-fix-this-iter as blocking. If you under-classify a wrong-decision finding to keep the loop's momentum, you are working against the project — wrong code accumulates faster than reviewers can catch it later. When in doubt, classify higher, not lower.
165	
166	## Return value
167	
168	Your final assistant message:
169	
170	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> files audited, <M> issues (critical/major/minor: a/b/c)`
171	- The path to your full report.
172	
173	## Reminders
174	
175	- **Read every file. No scope shortcuts.**
176	- **No strategic context.** Your audit is unbiased by design.
177	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
178	- **Excuse-comments are red flags.** Critical or major severity, every time.
179	- **Per-file checklist is the primary output.** Cover every file; the format is fixed.
180
I'll dispatch the mandatory critics and a lean-auditor in parallel — the user's "no wrong statements" directive needs the auditor's strategically-unbiased view.

882	        unfold cotangentExactSeqBeta
883	        exact ModuleCat.Derivation.desc_d _ _
884	      | zero => exact ⟨0, map_zero _⟩
885	      | add x y hx hy ih_x ih_y =>
886	        obtain ⟨a₁, ha₁⟩ := ih_x
887	        obtain ⟨a₂, ha₂⟩ := ih_y
888	        refine ⟨a₁ + a₂, ?_⟩
889	        rw [map_add, ha₁, ha₂]; rfl
890	      | smul a x hx ih =>
891	        obtain ⟨a', ha'⟩ := ih
892	        refine ⟨a • a', ?_⟩
893	        rw [map_smul, ha']; rfl
894	
895	/-- Cotangent exact sequence for a composition of schemes `X ⟶ Y ⟶ S`.
896	
897	For `f : X ⟶ Y` and `g : Y ⟶ S`, there is an exact sequence of
898	quasi-coherent `𝒪_X`-modules
899	```
900	  f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y} ⟶ 0.
901	```
902	Built on affine charts from the Mathlib ring-level cotangent exact
903	sequence (`KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map`) and glued via the
904	compatibility above.
905	
906	The three sub-declarations `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`, `cotangentExactSeqBeta`,
907	and `cotangentExactSeq_structure` carry the remaining sorries; this theorem
908	itself is closed by assembling them.
909	
910	The composition-zero proof obligation is now an explicit existential
911	witness rather than an in-type `by sorry`, eliminating the iter-064/065
912	nested `by sorry` placeholder inside `ShortComplex.mk`. -/
913	theorem cotangent_exact_sequence (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
914	    ∃ (α : (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (relativeDifferentials g) ⟶
915	        relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g))
916	      (β : relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g) ⟶ relativeDifferentials f)
917	      (h : α ≫ β = 0),
918	      CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.Exact
919	        (CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.mk α β h) ∧
920	      CategoryTheory.Epi β := by
921	  obtain ⟨h, hex, hep⟩ := cotangentExactSeq_structure f g
922	  exact ⟨_, _, h, hex, hep⟩
923	
924	/-! ## Smoothness and local freeness of `Ω` -/
925	
926	/-- Smoothness of a finite-presentation morphism is equivalent to `Ω_{X/S}`
927	being locally free of the given rank.
928	
929	The forward direction is the Jacobian criterion; the converse follows from
930	the cotangent exact sequence and Nakayama's lemma applied at each point. -/
931	theorem smooth_iff_locally_free_omega (f : X ⟶ S)
932	    (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f) (n : ℕ) :
933	    AlgebraicGeometry.IsSmoothOfRelativeDimension n f ↔
934	      ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : X.Opens), x ∈ U.1 ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
935	        let R := X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.obj (.op U)
936	        let M := (relativeDifferentials f).val.obj (.op U)
937	        Module.Free (↑R) (↑M) ∧ Module.rank (↑R) (↑M) = n := by
938	  sorry
939	
940	/-! ## Cotangent space at a section -/
941	
942	/-- If `f : X ⟶ S` is smooth and `s : S ⟶ X` is a section of `f`, then the
943	cotangent space `s^* Ω_{X/S}` is a locally free `𝒪_S`-module of rank `n`.
944	In particular, if `S = Spec k` and `X` is smooth of relative dimension `n`,
945	the cotangent space at the corresponding `k`-point is an `n`-dimensional
946	`k`-vector space. -/
947	theorem cotangent_at_section (f : X ⟶ S)
948	    (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f)
949	    (s : S ⟶ X) (hs : s ≫ f = 𝟙 S) (n : ℕ)
950	    (hsmooth : AlgebraicGeometry.IsSmoothOfRelativeDimension n f) :
951	    ∀ (x : S), ∃ (U : S.Opens), x ∈ U.1 ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
952	      let R := S.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.obj (.op U)
953	      let M := ((Scheme.Modules.pullback s).obj (relativeDifferentials f)).val.obj (.op U)
954	      Module.Free (↑R) (↑M) ∧ Module.rank (↑R) (↑M) = n := by
955	  sorry
956	
957	/-! ## Restriction of scalars to k-modules -/
958	
959	/-- The `O_C(U)`-module structure on `M(U)` viewed as a `k`-module via
960	restriction of scalars along the algebra map `k → O_C(U)`. -/
961	noncomputable abbrev moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj
962	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
963	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
964	    (M : C.left.Modules)
965	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) : ModuleCat.{u} k :=
966	  (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom).obj (M.val.obj U)
967	
968	/-- Smul-naturality lemma for `moduleKPresheafOfModules`: the additive map
969	`M.val.presheaf.map f` commutes with the `k`-action on source and target after
970	restriction of scalars. -/
971	lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_smul_compat
972	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
973	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
974	    (M : C.left.Modules)
975	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
976	    M.val.presheaf.map f ≫ (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V).smul r =
977	      (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U).smul r ≫ M.val.presheaf.map f := by
978	  -- Strategy: lift to ModuleCat.smul_naturality applied to (M.val.map f) over O_C, then transport
979	  -- the smul argument from `(kToSection C U).hom r` to `(kToSection C V).hom r` using algebraMap
980	  -- naturality.
981	  have hsmul_src : (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U).smul r =
982	      (M.val.obj U).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) := rfl
983	  have hsmul_tgt : (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V).smul r =
984	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r) := rfl
985	  rw [hsmul_src, hsmul_tgt]
986	  -- Now goal:
987	  --   M.val.presheaf.map f ≫ (M.val.obj V).smul (kV r)
988	  --     = (M.val.obj U).smul (kU r) ≫ M.val.presheaf.map f
989	  have hpres : M.val.presheaf.map f =
990	      (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat).map (M.val.map f) := by
991	    apply AddCommGrpCat.Hom.ext
992	    ext x
993	    exact PresheafOfModules.presheaf_map_apply_coe M.val f x
994	  rw [hpres]
995	  -- Now goal in terms of (forget₂).map (M.val.map f); use ModuleCat.smul_naturality:
996	  have hnat := ModuleCat.smul_naturality (M.val.map f) ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r)
997	  -- hnat has smul on the *target* of (M.val.map f) at index (kU r).
998	  -- That target is (restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (M.val.obj V), so its .smul (kU r)
999	  -- is definitionally (M.val.obj V).smul ((R.map f).hom (kU r)).

1050	  obj U := moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U
1051	  map f := moduleKPresheafOfModules_map C M f
1052	  map_id U := by
1053	    apply (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map_injective
1054	    rw [moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
1055	    exact M.val.presheaf.map_id U
1056	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
1057	    apply (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map_injective
1058	    rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp]
1059	    simp only [moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂]
1060	    exact M.val.presheaf.map_comp f g
1061	
1062	/-- The presheaf of `k`-modules obtained by restriction of scalars is a sheaf:
1063	its underlying presheaf of abelian groups coincides with that of the original
1064	`O_C`-module. -/
1065	lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_isSheaf
1066	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
1067	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
1068	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
1069	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
1070	      (moduleKPresheafOfModules C M) := by
1071	  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp _ _ (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} k) AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
1072	  convert M.isSheaf using 1
1073	
1074	/-- The sheaf of `k`-modules obtained from an `O_C`-module by restriction of
1075	scalars along the structure morphism. -/
1076	noncomputable def moduleKSheafOfModules
1077	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
1078	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
1079	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
1080	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
1081	  ⟨moduleKPresheafOfModules C M, moduleKPresheafOfModules_isSheaf C M⟩
1082	
1083	/-! ## Serre duality genus equality -/
1084	
1085	/-- For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over a field `k`,
1086	the dimension of `H^0(C, Ω_{C/k})` equals the dimension of `H^1(C, 𝒪_C)`,
1087	and both equal the genus `g(C)`.
1088	
1089	This is the dimension-one case of Serre duality applied to the canonical
1090	sheaf `ω_C = Ω_{C/k}`. -/
1091	theorem serre_duality_genus {k : Type u} [Field k]
1092	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom]
1093	    (hsmooth : Smooth C.hom) :
1094	    Module.rank k
1095	        (HModule k (moduleKSheafOfModules C (relativeDifferentials C.hom)) 0) =
1096	      Module.rank k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1) := by
1097	  sorry
1098	
1099	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Modules.Monoidal
8	
9	/-!
10	# Line bundles and the Picard group of a scheme
11	
12	Phase C step C1 (per `STRATEGY.md`, recipe `analogies/c1-route.md`).
13	
14	`LineBundle X` is the units of the (symmetric) monoidal-category skeleton of
15	`X.Modules`, mirroring the ring-side idiom
16	`CommRing.Pic R := Shrink (Skeleton (SemimoduleCat R))ˣ`
17	(`Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup:407`). The `CommGroup` structure derives by
18	typeclass resolution through `Skeleton.instCommMonoid`
19	(`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Skeleton:80`) and `instCommGroupUnits`.
20	
21	## Load-bearing transitive dependency (post-C1)
22	
23	The `BraidedCategory (X.Modules)` chain underwriting `instCommGroupLineBundle`
24	goes through `Localization.Monoidal.Braided`
25	(`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.Braided:118`), which needs
26	`(W X).IsMonoidal` — the project's named-deferred sorry
27	`instIsMonoidal_W` (`Modules/Monoidal.lean:166`). Post-C1, every theorem about
28	`LineBundle X`, `Pic X`, `Pic.pullback`, `PicardFunctor`, and the downstream
29	Jacobian arc transitively consumes that sorry.
30	
31	## Pull-back gap
32	
33	`Pic.pullback` body is `sorry`. It awaits closure of
34	`SheafOfModules.pullback_tensorObj` below — the named-deferred Mathlib gap
35	recording that `Scheme.Modules.pullback f : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` is monoidal.
36	Mathlib b80f227 has no `Functor.Monoidal` instance for this functor; the
37	project takes the named-deferral route per analogist option (c).
38	-/
39	
40	set_option autoImplicit false
41	
42	universe u
43	
44	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite MonoidalCategory
45	
46	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
47	
48	/-- A line bundle on a scheme `X`: an isomorphism class of invertible
49	quasi-coherent `O_X`-modules, packaged as the units of the skeleton of the
50	symmetric monoidal category `X.Modules`. Mirrors `CommRing.Pic R` which is
51	`Shrink (Skeleton (SemimoduleCat R))ˣ`. -/
52	def LineBundle (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type _ :=
53	  (Skeleton X.Modules)ˣ
54	
55	/-- Tensor product makes the type of line bundles a commutative group: the unit
56	is the structure sheaf `O_X`, multiplication is `⊗_{O_X}`, and the inverse of
57	a line bundle `L` is its dual `L^∨ = Hom_{O_X}(L, O_X)`.
58	
59	Derives via Lean's typeclass resolution from `BraidedCategory (X.Modules)`
60	through `Skeleton.instCommMonoid` and `instCommGroupUnits`. The
61	`BraidedCategory (X.Modules)` instance is provided by
62	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.instBraidedCategory` in
63	`Modules/Monoidal.lean`, transitively depending on `instIsMonoidal_W`. -/
64	noncomputable instance instCommGroupLineBundle (X : Scheme.{u}) :
65	    CommGroup (LineBundle X) :=
66	  inferInstanceAs (CommGroup (Skeleton X.Modules)ˣ)
67	
68	/-- The Picard group of a scheme: line bundles up to isomorphism. -/
69	abbrev Pic (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type _ := LineBundle X
70	
71	/-- **Mathlib gap (post-C1, named-deferred per Phase C1 default option (c))**.
72	The categorical pull-back functor `Scheme.Modules.pullback f : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules`
73	should be monoidal: `(pullback f).obj (M ⊗ N) ≅ (pullback f).obj M ⊗ (pullback f).obj N`
74	for every `M N : Y.Modules`. Mathlib b80f227 does not provide a
75	`Functor.Monoidal (Scheme.Modules.pullback f)` instance, so this iso must be
76	hand-built or named-deferred. The project takes the named-deferral route per
77	analogist `c1-route` (`analogies/c1-route.md`).
78	
79	A future Mathlib refresh that lands `(SheafOfModules.pullback _).Monoidal`
80	collapses this sorry to `MonoidalCategoryStruct.tensorObj_iso` (or the
81	canonical successor lemma). -/
82	noncomputable def SheafOfModules.pullback_tensorObj {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y)
83	    (M N : Y.Modules) :
84	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (M ⊗ N) ≅
85	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M ⊗ (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj N := by
86	  sorry
87	
88	/-- **Companion to `SheafOfModules.pullback_tensorObj`**: the categorical pull-back
89	functor `Scheme.Modules.pullback f` preserves the monoidal unit (the structure
90	sheaf). This is the unit-preservation half of the monoidality of
91	`Scheme.Modules.pullback f` — formally part of the same Mathlib gap as
92	`pullback_tensorObj` above. Recorded as a separate `sorry` here so that
93	`Pic.pullback` may be fully built from these two oracles. A future
94	Mathlib refresh landing `(SheafOfModules.pullback _).Monoidal` collapses
95	this to `Monoidal.εIso` (or the canonical successor). -/
96	noncomputable def SheafOfModules.pullback_oneIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) :
97	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (𝟙_ Y.Modules) ≅ 𝟙_ X.Modules := by
98	  sorry
99	
100	/-- Pull-back of line bundles along a scheme morphism, as a group homomorphism.
101	The eventual closed form is
102	`Units.map (Skeleton.monoidHom (Scheme.Modules.pullback f))`, requiring a
103	`Functor.Monoidal` instance on `Scheme.Modules.pullback f` — currently a
104	Mathlib gap. We hand-build the underlying monoid hom by routing
105	`(Scheme.Modules.pullback f).mapSkeleton` through the unit-preservation iso
106	`SheafOfModules.pullback_oneIso` and the tensor-preservation iso
107	`SheafOfModules.pullback_tensorObj`. -/
108	noncomputable def Pic.pullback {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) :
109	    Pic Y →* Pic X :=
110	  Units.map
111	    { toFun := (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).mapSkeleton.obj
112	      map_one' := by
113	        change (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).mapSkeleton.obj
114	          (toSkeleton (𝟙_ Y.Modules)) = toSkeleton (𝟙_ X.Modules)
115	        rw [Functor.mapSkeleton_obj_toSkeleton]
116	        exact congr_toSkeleton_of_iso (SheafOfModules.pullback_oneIso f)
117	      map_mul' := fun a b => by
118	        change (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).mapSkeleton.obj
119	            (toSkeleton (a.out ⊗ b.out)) =
120	          toSkeleton
121	            (((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).mapSkeleton.obj a).out ⊗
122	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).mapSkeleton.obj b).out)
123	        rw [Functor.mapSkeleton_obj_toSkeleton]
124	        refine congr_toSkeleton_of_iso ?_
125	        exact (SheafOfModules.pullback_tensorObj f a.out b.out).trans
126	          ((fromSkeletonToSkeletonIso _).symm ⊗ᵢ
127	            (fromSkeletonToSkeletonIso _).symm) }
128	
129	/-- Pull-back of line bundles along the identity is the identity homomorphism. -/
130	@[simp]
131	lemma Pic.pullback_id (X : Scheme.{u}) : Pic.pullback (𝟙 X) = MonoidHom.id (Pic X) := by
132	  ext u
133	  apply Units.ext
134	  change (Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 X)).mapSkeleton.obj u.val = u.val
135	  -- mapSkeleton.obj u.val = toSkeleton ((pullback (𝟙 X)).obj u.val.out)
136	  -- and (pullback (𝟙 X)).obj u.val.out ≅ u.val.out (via Scheme.Modules.pullbackId)
137	  -- so toSkeleton ((pullback (𝟙 X)).obj u.val.out) = toSkeleton u.val.out = u.val.
138	  have iso : (Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 X)).obj u.val.out ≅ u.val.out :=
139	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X).app u.val.out
140	  change toSkeleton ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 X)).obj u.val.out) = u.val
141	  rw [congr_toSkeleton_of_iso iso]
142	  exact Quotient.out_eq _
143	
144	/-- Pull-back of line bundles is contravariantly functorial:
145	`(f ≫ g)^* = f^* ∘ g^*` on Picard groups. -/
146	@[simp]
147	lemma Pic.pullback_comp {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) :
148	    Pic.pullback (f ≫ g) = (Pic.pullback f).comp (Pic.pullback g) := by
149	  ext u
150	  apply Units.ext
151	  -- LHS: toSkeleton ((pullback (f ≫ g)).obj u.val.out)
152	  -- RHS: toSkeleton ((pullback f).obj (toSkeleton ((pullback g).obj u.val.out)).out)
153	  -- Via Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp : pullback g ⋙ pullback f ≅ pullback (f ≫ g),
154	  -- (pullback (f ≫ g)).obj u.val.out ≅ (pullback f).obj ((pullback g).obj u.val.out)
155	  -- ≅ (pullback f).obj ((toSkeleton ((pullback g).obj u.val.out)).out).
156	  change toSkeleton ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (f ≫ g)).obj u.val.out) =
157	    toSkeleton ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj
158	      ((toSkeleton ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj u.val.out)).out))
159	  refine congr_toSkeleton_of_iso ?_
160	  refine ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f g).symm.app u.val.out).trans ?_
161	  exact (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).mapIso
162	    (fromSkeletonToSkeletonIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj u.val.out)).symm
163	
164	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
165

130	over the stalk ring. Tensoring with the identity preserves isos in any monoidal category.
131	Combined with `WEqualsLocallyBijective` (an iso after sheafification ⟺ locally bijective
132	⟺ stalkwise iso), this gives `IsIso (L (f ▷ Y₁))` from `IsIso (L f)`.
133	
134	The blocker is the precise Mathlib identification:
135	`(M ⊗_psh N).stalk x ≅ M.stalk x ⊗_{R₀.stalk x} N.stalk x`
136	(stalks-of-presheaf-tensor = tensor-of-stalks, over the stalk ring, in the varying-ring
137	setting). Mathlib has the analogous statement for fixed-ring `ModuleCat R` (see
138	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Tilde.lean`), and stalks-commute-with-colimits is
139	abstractly available, but the explicit identification for `PresheafOfModules R₀` with
140	varying `R₀` is absent from `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Sheafify.lean`
141	and the surrounding modules — searches via `lean_leansearch` for "stalk presheaf tensor"
142	and grep for "stalk.*tensor" in `Mathlib/Algebra/Category` return nothing.
143	
144	Alternative routes investigated and ruled out (preserved from iter-079):
145	- `tensorHom_def` decomposition alone is circular at the abstract level — it reduces to
146	  the same whiskerLeft/whiskerRight closure question.
147	- `sheafificationCompToSheaf` (sheafification of `PresheafOfModules` = sheafification of
148	  underlying abelian-grp presheaves) gives an iso-detection in `AddCommGrpCat`, but the
149	  tensor products on the two sides do not commute (`PresheafOfModules` tensor is
150	  $R_0(U)$-balanced, `AddCommGrpCat` tensor is $\mathbb{Z}$-balanced), so the iso-stability
151	  question on the abelian-grp side does not transfer to the module side under tensor.
152	- Closedness via internal hom requires `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)`, which
153	  Mathlib provides only for fixed-ring `ModuleCat R`
154	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Closed`), not for varying-ring
155	  `PresheafOfModules R₀`. A multi-PR Mathlib upstream effort would be needed.
156	
157	Marked sorry until the upstream gap (stalk-of-presheaf-tensor in the varying-ring setting)
158	is filled. Per user policy 2026-05-11, no project-local helper lemma may be introduced to
159	bridge it — the closure must remain Mathlib-only.
160	
161	Note: this sorry does NOT block downstream consumers, which use `MonoidalCategory X.Modules`
162	(set up by `instMonoidalCategoryStruct` + `instMonoidalCategory` below, both fully closed
163	iter-079 via `LocalizedMonoidal`). Only consumers that take `(W X).IsMonoidal` as an
164	explicit hypothesis would be affected, and there are none in the project. Phase C step C1
165	(LineBundle refinement) is unblocked. -/
166	noncomputable instance instIsMonoidal_W : (W X).IsMonoidal := by
167	  -- `IsMonoidal.mk'` reduces to: if `L f` and `L g` are isos in `X.Modules`,
168	  -- then `L (f ⊗ₘ g)` is also iso. Substantive content: iso-stability of
169	  -- sheafification under presheaf-tensor whiskering. See docstring.
170	  refine MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal.mk' _ fun {X₁ X₂ Y₁ Y₂} f g hf hg => ?_
171	  simp only [MorphismProperty.inverseImage_iff,
172	    MorphismProperty.isomorphisms.iff] at hf hg ⊢
173	  sorry
174	
175	/-- The symmetric monoidal category structure on `X.Modules`: derived from the
176	`LocalizedMonoidal` machinery applied to the sheafification functor
177	`L : PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⥤ X.Modules` at the class `W X` of morphisms
178	inverted by `L`, with unit `L.obj (𝟙_ presheaf)` and `ε := Iso.refl _`.
179	
180	The localization's `tensorObj` is `((tensorBifunctor L W ε).obj M).obj N`, which is
181	naturally isomorphic to (but not definitionally equal to) the `tensorObj` defined above

## Slug

iter117

## Iteration

117

## What the user just asked the planner

The user wrote a fresh USER_HINTS.md (transcribed verbatim):

> enumerated all of the achievements in the previous steps, nothing should be

This is a fresh, substantive direction. The planner is composing a brand-new
STRATEGY.md to replace the current one and needs your critique BEFORE
committing it. Specifically the planner wants you to:

(a) audit the current STRATEGY.md (attached below) against the four
    user-stated criteria (autonomy, strict correctness, blueprint depth,
    clean organization, no deferrals),
(b) identify which parts of the current strategy stand under the new
    directive and which collapse,
(c) recommend a high-level shape for the replacement strategy
    consistent with the directive,
(d) name any specific Mathlib infrastructure gaps the planner must
    plan to fill rather than defer.

## Project goal (one paragraph)

The project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically
irreducible curve over a field `k`, following Christian Merten's challenge
file `references/challenge.lean`. The 9 declarations in
`archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables: `AlgebraicGeometry.genus`,
`AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian`, the four `Jacobian` instances (`GrpObj`,
`SmoothOfRelativeDimension genus`, `IsProper`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`),
`Jacobian.ofCurve` (Abel-Jacobi), `Jacobian.comp_ofCurve`, and
`Jacobian.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` (Albanese universal property).

## Current state (project total: 16 inline `sorry` sites)

Verified `sorry_analyzer.py` distribution:

- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean`: 6 (L1120, L1212,
  L1536, L1564, L1754, L1846).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean`: 5 (L191, L737, L931, L947, L1091).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean`: 1 (L173).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundle.lean`: 2 (L82, L96).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean`: 1 (L181).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean`: 1 (L179).

Dependency-graph reality (verified `dependency_graph.py`): the protected
chain depends transitively on ONLY ONE of these sorries —
`nonempty_jacobianWitness` at `Jacobian.lean:179`. The other 15 sorries
are project-content orphans (Differentials cotangent API, Picard arc,
Čech basic-open machinery, monoidal `X.Modules` framework). Removing them
does not break a single protected declaration.

## References summary

`references/summary.md`:

> | File | Description |
> | ---- | ----------- |
> | `challenge.lean` | Original AI challenge file by Christian Merten — the
> formal statement of the missing definitions and theorems for the Jacobian
> of an algebraic curve. The Lean skeleton in `AlgebraicJacobian/` is a
> decomposition of this file; signatures here are authoritative. |

## Blueprint chapter index

(One-line topic per chapter from `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`.)

- `Rigidity.tex` — Mumford rigidity for pointed proper smooth morphisms.
- `Genus.tex` — Definition of `genus C := dim_k H^1(C, O_C)`.
- `Jacobian.tex` — `Jacobian C` via Albanese witness; the four `Jacobian`
  instances; `IsAlbanese` and the `JacobianWitness` structure.
- `AbelJacobi.tex` — `Jacobian.ofCurve`, `comp_ofCurve`,
  `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`.
- `Cohomology_SheafCompose.tex` — Composition of sheaves with forgetful
  functors (project-local API).
- `Cohomology_StructureSheafAb.tex` — `O_X` viewed as an abelian-group
  sheaf.
- `Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex` — `O_X` viewed as a `k`-module
  sheaf (for the `Genus` definition).
- `Cohomology_MayerVietorisCore.tex` /
  `Cohomology_MayerVietorisCover.tex` — Mayer-Vietoris foundations + the
  cover construction (project-local Čech infrastructure).
- `Differentials.tex` — Relative Kähler differentials `Ω_{X/S}` of a
  morphism of schemes, the cotangent exact sequence, smoothness ↔ local
  freeness of `Ω`, and the Serre-duality genus equality.
- `Modules_Monoidal.tex` — Monoidal structure on `X.Modules` via
  sheafification localization.
- `Picard_LineBundle.tex` — `LineBundle X` as units of `Skeleton X.Modules`;
  `Pic.pullback`.
- `Picard_Functor.tex` — Picard functor and representability.
- `Picard_FunctorAb.tex` — Abelian-group lift of the Picard functor.

```markdown
{strategy_md}
```

## What the planner is considering for the replacement

The planner is leaning toward this high-level reshape:

1. **The 9 protected declarations are mathematically correct as
   currently formulated.** None of them are "temporarily wrong"; the
   only sorry on the protected chain is `nonempty_jacobianWitness`,
   whose statement is the true existence of a Jacobian/Albanese variety
   for a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve.

2. **The 15 orphan project-content sorries split into two groups**:
   - **Closeable with bounded infrastructure** (~1–10 iters each):
     L1846 (`h_loc_exact`, mechanizable from existing Mathlib per the
     iter-108 audit), L82/L96 (`pullback_tensorObj`/`pullback_oneIso`,
     hand-build of the monoidal-functor witness on
     `Scheme.Modules.pullback f`), L931/L947 (`smooth_iff_locally_free_omega`
     forward/converse via `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential`
     [verified] + `AlgebraicGeometry.isSmoothOfRelativeDimension_iff`
     [verified]), L737 (`h_exact`, requires building
     `SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise`, ~2–4 iters), L191
     (`isSheafUniqueGluing_type`, requires either Mathlib-bridge build or
     refactor to presheaf-only framing).

   - **Genuinely massive Mathlib gaps** (multi-thousand-LOC builds, each
     comparable to a Hartshorne chapter):
     - L1091 `serre_duality_genus` — dualizing sheaf, trace morphism for
       proper morphisms, duality pairing.
     - L181 `PicardFunctor.representable` — FGA representability of the
       Picard functor.
     - L173 `instIsMonoidal_W` — `stalk_tensorObj` for varying-ring
       `PresheafOfModules` (Mathlib only has the fixed-ring version).
     - L179 `nonempty_jacobianWitness` — Albanese existence, requires
       Hilbert/Quot schemes + finite-group scheme quotients (or
       alternative routes like the divisor-class-image Pic⁰
       construction).
     - The Phase A residual `BasicOpenCech.lean` sorries (L1120, L1212,
       L1536, L1564, L1754) — load-bearing Čech-cohomology body work
       gated on the L1120 `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` route which has been
       PAUSED for 4+ iters per progress-critic STUCK verdicts.

3. **User directive "nothing deferred" leaves three honest options for
   the massive gaps**:
   - **(α) Build the prerequisite Mathlib infrastructure** within the
     loop. Plan multi-thousand-LOC sub-projects. Likely 50+ iters and
     out of practical scope for the autonomous loop.
   - **(β) Find alternative routes that bypass the missing
     infrastructure**. For some (e.g. L173 has no obvious bypass since
     it's the actual mathematical content), this option is closed.
     For others (L179 Jacobian-existence has the divisor-class-image
     Pic⁰ alternative; L181 has direct construction routes), this is
     viable but each route also requires real Mathlib gap-fills.
   - **(γ) Remove the corresponding declaration from project scope**.
     Since 15 of 16 sorries are orphan project content, removing them
     does not break the protected chain. The blueprint would need to be
     trimmed accordingly. This is mathematically honest (we ship less,
     but everything we ship is closed).

4. **The planner's current best guess** is a hybrid:
   - Close all closeable sorries (L1846, L82, L96, L931, L947, L737,
     L191 via refactor) — 6 sorries closed across ~10–15 iters.
   - For L1091, L181, L173: pre-commit to one of (α), (β), (γ) per

     blueprint sections trimmed.
   - For L179: this is the load-bearing protected-chain sorry. Removing
     it would require deleting the Jacobian definition itself; the
     user's directive is to find a strategy, not to abandon the project
     goal. The cleanest plan is (β) — build the divisor-class-image
     Pic⁰ alternative route, OR honestly disclose that the Jacobian
     construction is the headline open problem.
   - The Phase A residual `BasicOpenCech` sorries are orphan and not
     transitively needed by `Genus.lean`'s definition (which uses the
     project-local `HModule` not Čech machinery). Option (γ) on those
     — strip the Phase A work to only what `MayerVietorisCover.lean`
     genuinely needs (which is 0 sorries; it already compiles).

## What you should produce

A standard strategy-critic report covering:

- **Routes audited** (the three options α/β/γ above as candidate
  responses to the user directive; or new options if you identify
  better ones).
- **Verdicts per option**: PROCEED / CHALLENGE / REJECT with reasons.
- **Sunk-cost / momentum flags** on the current strategy.
- **Recommended replacement-strategy shape** for the planner. Be

The planner specifically wants you to challenge: is the directive
"nothing deferred" compatible with shipping a non-trivial fraction of

1	\chapter{The sheaf of relative differentials}
2	\label{chap:differentials}
3	
4	This chapter develops the sheaf of relative K\"ahler differentials $\Omega_{X/S}$ of a morphism of schemes $X \to S$, starting from the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{B/A}$ already available in Mathlib. The output is a quasi-coherent sheaf of $\struct{X}$-modules characterised by its restriction to affine opens: for any affine open $V = \Spec B \subseteq X$ lying over an affine open $U = \Spec A \subseteq S$, the sections satisfy
5	\[
6	  \Omega_{X/S}(V) \;=\; \Omega_{B/A}.
7	\]
8	
9	The downstream role of this chapter is twofold. First, the smoothness hypothesis on the curve $C \to \Spec k$ that appears in the Jacobian definition and the Abel--Jacobi map amounts to the condition that $\Omega_{C/k}$ is locally free of rank one; this characterisation is what unlocks the dimension computation $g(C) = \dim_k H^0(C, \Omega_{C/k}) = \dim_k H^1(C, \struct{C})$. Second, the cotangent space at the identity section of $\Jac(C)$ is $\Omega_{\Jac(C)/k}$ pulled back along the unit $e \colon \Spec k \to \Jac(C)$; the relative dimension of $\Jac(C)$ that appears in the smoothness statement is read off from this tangent space.
10	
11	\section{The relative cotangent sheaf}
12	
13	\begin{definition}\leanok
14	[Relative cotangent presheaf of a morphism]
15	  \label{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
16	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}
17	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes. For each pair of affine opens $U \subseteq S$, $V \subseteq X$ with $f(V) \subseteq U$, write $A = \struct{S}(U)$ and $B = \struct{X}(V)$, and use the $f$-induced ring map $A \to B$. The K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{B/A}$ is then a $B$-module, hence a section over $V$ of a presheaf on $X$. The collection of these modules, together with the natural restriction maps along inclusions of affine opens, defines a presheaf of $\struct{X}$-modules on $X$.
18	\end{definition}
19	
20	\begin{lemma}\leanok
21	[Unique-gluing form of the sheaf condition for $\Omega_{X/S}$]
22	  \label{lem:relative_kaehler_isSheafUniqueGluing}
23	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheafUniqueGluing_type}
24	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
25	  The underlying type-valued presheaf of the relative cotangent presheaf
26	  $\Omega_{X/S}$ satisfies the unique-gluing characterisation of the
27	  sheaf condition (\texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheafUniqueGluing}): for
28	  every family of opens $U \colon \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)$ and every
29	  compatible family of K\"ahler differentials
30	  $sf_i \in \Omega_{X/S}(U_i)$, there is a unique
31	  $s \in \Omega_{X/S}(\bigvee_i U_i)$ whose restriction to each $U_i$
32	  equals $sf_i$.
33	\end{lemma}
34	
35	\begin{proof}
36	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf, def:universal_derivation}
37	  \leanok
38	  Write $F = (\Omega_{X/S})^{\sharp}$ for the underlying type-valued
39	  presheaf and fix a compatible family
40	  $(sf_i)_{i \in \iota}$ with $sf_i \in \Omega_{X/S}(U_i)$ as above.
41	  The proof reduces the unique-gluing claim to an affine-basis-restricted
42	  sheaf condition that the project supplies by hand, with no off-the-shelf
43	  Mathlib bridge available.
44	
45	  \emph{Step 1: Affine-basis sheaf condition.} The scheme $X$ admits the
46	  canonical basis of affine opens
47	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isBasis\_affineOpens} [verified]
48	  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme}). For each affine
49	  $V_\alpha = \Spec B_\alpha \subseteq X$ lying over an affine
50	  $W_\alpha = \Spec A_\alpha \subseteq S$ and each basic open
51	  $D(g) \subseteq V_\alpha$ (for $g \in B_\alpha$), the presheaf
52	  satisfies
53	  \[
54	    F(D(g)) \;=\; \Omega_{B_\alpha[1/g]/A_\alpha}
55	    \;\cong\; \Omega_{B_\alpha/A_\alpha} \otimes_{B_\alpha} B_\alpha[1/g],
56	  \]
57	  where the isomorphism is the canonical localisation map of
58	  K\"ahler differentials provided by
59	  \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule} [verified]
60	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.TensorProduct}) applied with multiplicative
61	  submonoid $\mathrm{Submonoid.powers}\,g$ and target ring
62	  $B_\alpha[1/g]$. Equivalently the bare existence of this iso is packaged
63	  as \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquivOfFormallyEtale}
64	  [verified] applied to $A_\alpha \to B_\alpha \to B_\alpha[1/g]$
65	  (valid because $B_\alpha \to B_\alpha[1/g]$ is formally \'etale, by
66	  \texttt{Algebra.FormallyEtale.of\_isLocalization} [verified]).
67	
68	  Under this identification, the restriction of $F$ to the basis of basic
69	  opens of $V_\alpha$ matches the basis-restriction of Mathlib's
70	  quasi-coherent module sheaf $\widetilde{\Omega_{B_\alpha/A_\alpha}}$ on
71	  $V_\alpha = \Spec B_\alpha$. The sheaf $\widetilde{\,\cdot\,}$ is
72	  built-in as
73	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.tilde} [verified]
74	  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde}); its
75	  \texttt{isSheaf} field provides the sheaf property as part of the
76	  construction (internally via
77	  \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf\_iff\_isSheaf\_comp} applied to
78	  \texttt{structureSheafInType}). In particular, on the basis of basic
79	  opens of each affine chart $V_\alpha$ the presheaf $F$ already
80	  satisfies the equalizer-products / unique-gluing characterisation of
81	  the sheaf condition --- internally to that affine chart.
82	
83	  \emph{Step 2: Hand-rolled cofinality descent.} For the family
84	  $U \colon \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)$ refine to a basis-indexed cover
85	  $U' \colon \iota' \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)$ by intersecting each $U_i$ with
86	  the affine basis: for each $i$ and each affine $V_\alpha$ that meets
87	  $U_i$ and each basic open $D(g) \subseteq V_\alpha \cap U_i$, include
88	  $D(g)$ in the refined family. The refined cover $U'$ is \emph{cofinal} in
89	  the original cover $U$ inside the \texttt{OpensLeCover}-comma category
90	  over $\bigvee_i U_i$ --- every member of $U'$ sits inside some member of
91	  $U$, and the basic-open basis is dense in the Opens-topology
92	  (\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isBasis\_affineOpens} +
93	  \texttt{Opens.isBasis\_iff\_nbhd}).
94	
95	  By Step~1, the family $(sf_i)$ pulls back along the refinement
96	  $\bigsqcup_j U'_j \to \bigsqcup_i U_i$ to a compatible family on
97	  $U'$ that lies entirely on basic opens of affine charts; by the
98	  $\widetilde{\,\cdot\,}$-sheaf property on each $V_\alpha$, this
99	  refined-family compatibility produces a unique gluing on
100	  $\bigvee_j U'_j = \bigvee_i U_i$, yielding a unique section
101	  $s \in F(\bigvee_i U_i)$. Cofinality in the \texttt{OpensLeCover}
102	  category guarantees that the equalizer-products condition on $U'$
103	  transfers back to the original $U$. The structural target of this
104	  cofinality step is the four-fold equivalent characterisation
105	  \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf\_iff\_isSheafOpensLeCover} [verified]
106	  + \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf\_iff\_isSheafUniqueGluing\_types}
107	  [verified]; the present lemma is stated in the unique-gluing form
108	  because it is the form the iter-113 Lean target asks for, but the
109	  load-bearing descent argument is the same in any of the four
110	  equivalent forms.
111	
112	  \emph{[gap]} No off-the-shelf Mathlib lemma packages
113	  ``\texttt{Scheme.PresheafOfModules}-sheaf-on-affine-basis $\Rightarrow$
114	  sheaf on $X$''; the cofinality descent above is hand-rolled this
115	  iter, and the iter-115 prover must build it inline (verified by the
116	  iter-114 Mathlib analogist: the bridge is genuinely missing from
117	  Mathlib b80f227, even after considering
118	  \texttt{Functor.IsCoverDense}, \texttt{IsDenseSubsite}, and the
119	  1-hypercover-dense infrastructure).
120	
121	  \emph{Step 3: Uniqueness.} If $s, s' \in F(\bigvee_i U_i)$ both
122	  restrict to $sf_i$ on each $U_i$, then $s - s'$ restricts to zero
123	  on each $U_i$; by Step~1's affine-basis identification this
124	  $s - s'$ restricts to zero on each basic open $D(g) \subseteq U'_j$
125	  of the refined cover; by the $\widetilde{\,\cdot\,}$-sheaf property
126	  on each affine chart, $s - s' = 0$ on the supremum
127	  $\bigvee_j U'_j = \bigvee_i U_i$. The equivalent uniqueness
128	  argument runs through
129	  \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.Sheaf.eq\_of\_locally\_eq} [verified]
130	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.SheafCondition.UniqueGluing})
131	  applied to the structure sheaf and reduced via
132	  \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.span\_range\_derivation} [verified]
133	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic}) to spanning generators.
134	\end{proof}
135	
136	\begin{theorem}\leanok
137	[Sheaf condition for $\Omega_{X/S}$]
138	  \label{thm:relative_kaehler_isSheaf}
139	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf}
140	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
141	  The relative cotangent presheaf is a sheaf in the Zariski topology on $X$.
142	\end{theorem}
143	
144	\begin{proof}
145	  \uses{lem:relative_kaehler_isSheafUniqueGluing, def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
146	  \leanok
147	  By Mathlib's \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf\_iff\_isSheaf\_comp}
148	  [verified] (\texttt{Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.Forget}) applied to the
149	  forgetful chain
150	  \[
151	    \mathrm{ModuleCat}\,\struct{X} \longrightarrow \mathrm{AddCommGrp}
152	      \longrightarrow \mathrm{Type},
153	  \]
154	  whose composite preserves limits and reflects isomorphisms because
155	  each factor does, it suffices to verify the sheaf condition on the
156	  underlying type-valued presheaf of
157	  $\mathrm{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}\,f$. Mathlib's
158	  \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf\_of\_isSheafUniqueGluing\_types}
159	  [verified] reduces this further to the unique-gluing condition, which
160	  is the content of Lemma~\ref{lem:relative_kaehler_isSheafUniqueGluing}.
161	\end{proof}
162	
163	\begin{definition}\leanok
164	[Relative cotangent sheaf $\Omega_{X/S}$]
165	  \label{def:relative_kaehler_sheaf}
166	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentials}
167	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf, thm:relative_kaehler_isSheaf}
168	  The sheaf of $\struct{X}$-modules associated with the relative cotangent presheaf is the \emph{sheaf of relative differentials} $\Omega_{X/S}$. The Lean object is quasi-coherent on each affine chart by construction (the presheaf is locally a Mathlib \texttt{KaehlerDifferential} module); the \texttt{IsQuasicoherent} typeclass on the assembled sheaf is not yet registered.
169	\end{definition}
170	
171	\begin{remark}[Comparison with Mathlib's ring-level $\Omega_{B/A}$]
172	  \label{rem:kahler_compatibility}
173	  Mathlib provides the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{B/A}$ together with the universal derivation $\mathrm{d} \colon B \to \Omega_{B/A}$. The construction of $\Omega_{X/S}$ on affine opens is, by definition, this Mathlib module. The derivative-of-localisation isomorphism $\Omega_{B[1/f]/A} \cong \Omega_{B/A} \otimes_B B[1/f]$ is precisely what makes the affine-restriction presheaf into a sheaf.
174	\end{remark}
175	
176	\section{Universal property and the cotangent exact sequence}
177	
178	\begin{definition}\leanok
179	[Universal derivation]
180	  \label{def:universal_derivation}

351	/-! ## Universal derivation -/
352	
353	/-- The universal derivation `d : 𝒪_X → Ω_{X/S}`.
354	
355	On each affine open `V = Spec B` over `Spec A`, this is the Mathlib universal
356	derivation `d_{B/A} : B → Ω_{B/A}`. It is an `A`-linear sheaf map satisfying
357	the Leibniz rule `d(ab) = a d(b) + b d(a)`. -/
358	noncomputable def universalDerivation (f : X ⟶ S) :
359	    X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat ⟶
360	      (relativeDifferentials f).val.presheaf := by
361	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
362	    f.c
363	  let d' := PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ'
364	  have h_eq : X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat := rfl
365	  refine {
366	    app := fun U ↦ AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (d'.d (X := U)),
367	    naturality := fun U V g ↦ ?naturality
368	  }
369	  case naturality =>
370	    ext x
371	    simp only [sheafCompose_obj_obj, PresheafOfModules.presheaf_obj_coe, Functor.comp_obj,
372	      CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj, Functor.comp_map, AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp]
373	    suffices d'.d ((ConcreteCategory.hom (X.presheaf.map g)) x) =
374	        (ConcreteCategory.hom ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).map g)) (d'.d x) by
375	      simpa using this
376	    exact d'.d_map g x

378	/-! ## Cotangent exact sequence
379	
380	The cotangent exact sequence
381	```
382	  f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y} ⟶ 0
383	```
384	for a composition `X ⟶ Y ⟶ S` is decomposed into five named sub-claims:
385	
386	1. `cotangentExactSeqAlpha` — the base-change cotangent map
387	   `f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S}`. Locally, this is
388	   `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange A A' B`, where `A → A' → B` is the
389	   tower of structure ring maps on an affine chart.
390	2. `cotangentExactSeqBeta` — the relative-quotient cotangent map
391	   `Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y}`. Locally, this is the surjection
392	   `KaehlerDifferential.map A A' B B` induced by the inclusion `A → A'`
393	   that kills the `A`-linear derivations factoring through `A'`.
394	3. `cotangentExactSeqAlpha_comp_Beta` — composition zero `α ≫ β = 0`,
395	   from the ring-level `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` (the
396	   range of `mapBaseChange` lies in the kernel of `map`).
397	4. `cotangentExactSeqShortComplexExact` — exactness `ker β = im α`,
398	   from the same ring-level theorem and gluing.
399	5. `cotangentExactSeqBetaEpi` — `β` is an epi, from
400	   `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective` applied on each affine chart.
401	
402	The headline theorem `cotangent_exact_sequence` then assembles the
403	existential bundle from these five components without further sorries. -/

408	/-- The base-change cotangent map `f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S}`.

410	For a composition `X ⟶ Y ⟶ S`, this is the natural map whose local
411	description on an affine chart with rings `A = O_S(W)`, `A' = O_Y(f(V))`,
412	`B = O_X(V)` is `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange A A' B`:
413	```
414	  Ω_{A'/A} ⊗_{A'} B ⟶ Ω_{B/A}
415	```
416	sending `da ⊗ b ↦ b · da` (where the right-hand `da` is the universal
417	derivation in `Ω_{B/A}`).
418

420	base-change maps, mediated by `PresheafOfModules.homMk` applied to the

422	noncomputable def cotangentExactSeqAlpha (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
423	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (relativeDifferentials g) ⟶
424	      relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g) := by
425	  -- Strategy. Apply the `pullback ⊣ pushforward` adjunction to convert the goal into the
426	  -- adjoint hom on `Y`, then build that hom from its underlying presheaf-of-modules morphism via
427	  -- the universal property of `relativeDifferentials' φ_g'`.
428	  --
429	  -- After `homEquiv.symm`, the goal becomes
430	  --   `relativeDifferentials g ⟶
431	  --      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj (relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g))`,
432	  -- both `Y.Modules`. Their underlying presheaves are
433	  --   LHS.val = relativeDifferentialsPresheaf g
434	  --           = PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ_g'
435	  --   RHS.val = (PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj
436	  --               (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g))
437	  -- so it suffices to build a `PresheafOfModules` morphism between the underlying values, then
438	  -- package via `⟨·⟩` as a `SheafOfModules.Hom`.
439	  refine ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _).symm ?_
440	  -- Set up the two ring presheaf morphisms that drive the relativeDifferentials' construction.
441	  let φ_g' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat g.base).homEquiv
442	    S.presheaf Y.presheaf).symm g.c
443	  let φ_fg' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).homEquiv
444	    S.presheaf X.presheaf).symm (f ≫ g).c
445	  let D_X := PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_fg'
446	  let adj_f := TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base
447	  let d_target :
448	      ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj
449	        (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g))).Derivation' φ_g' :=
450	    { d := fun {U} => AddMonoidHom.mk' (fun b => D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom b))
451	        (fun a b => by
452	          change D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom (a + b)) =
453	            D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom a) + D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom b)
454	          rw [show (f.c.app U).hom (a + b) = (f.c.app U).hom a + (f.c.app U).hom b
455	                from map_add _ _ _]
456	          exact map_add D_X.d _ _)
457	      d_mul := by
458	        intro U a b
459	        dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
460	        rw [show (f.c.app U).hom (a * b) = (f.c.app U).hom a * (f.c.app U).hom b
461	              from map_mul _ _ _]
462	        exact D_X.d_mul _ _
463	      d_map := by
464	        intro U V f' x
465	        dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
466	        have hnat' : (f.c.app V).hom (Y.presheaf.map f' x) =
467	            X.presheaf.map ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).map f'.unop).op
468	              ((f.c.app U).hom x) := by
469	          have h := congr_arg (fun h : _ ⟶ _ => (ConcreteCategory.hom h) x) (f.c.naturality f')
470	          simpa using h
471	        erw [hnat']
472	        exact D_X.d_map _ _
473	      d_app := by
474	        intro U a
475	        -- Adjunction-coherence (iter-078 closure): the goal after unfolding the
476	        -- AddMonoidHom.mk' wrapper is
477	        --   `D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom (φ_g'.app U a)) = 0`.
478	        -- We build a Y-presheaf bridge
479	        --   τ : (pullback g).obj S.presheaf ⟶
480	        --         (pushforward f).obj ((pullback (f≫g)).obj S.presheaf)
481	        -- (dual to the η-bridge used in `cotangentExactSeqBeta`) via
482	        --   τ := adj_g.homEquiv.symm (adj_fg.unit.app S.presheaf).
483	        -- Then we prove the coherence
484	        --   τ ≫ (pushforward f).map φ_fg' = φ_g' ≫ f.c
485	        -- by applying adj_g.homEquiv to both sides (both collapse to (f≫g).c via the
486	        -- two homEquiv_symm/apply identities and the rfl-level composition law
487	        -- (f≫g).c = g.c ≫ (pushforward g).map f.c).  Reading the coherence at U
488	        -- factors `(f.c.app U).hom (φ_g'.app U a)` through `φ_fg'.app _`, killing the
489	        -- derivation D_X.d.
490	        dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
491	        -- The bridge τ.
492	        let τ : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat g.base).obj S.presheaf ⟶
493	              (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).obj
494	                ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).obj S.presheaf) :=
495	          ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat g.base).homEquiv
496	              S.presheaf _).symm
497	            ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).unit.app
498	              S.presheaf)
499	        -- Coherence: τ ≫ (pushforward f).map φ_fg' = φ_g' ≫ f.c, by injectivity of
500	        -- adj_g.homEquiv plus the two homEquiv/Equiv.apply_symm_apply collapses.
501	        have hτ : τ ≫ (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ_fg' =
502	            φ_g' ≫ f.c := by
503	          apply ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat g.base).homEquiv
504	            _ _).injective
505	          rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right,
506	              Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right]
507	          -- LHS: homEquiv τ ≫ pushforward g.map (pushforward f.map φ_fg')
508	          -- RHS: homEquiv φ_g' ≫ pushforward g.map f.c
509	          -- Both reduce to (f≫g).c.
510	          have eq1 : ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat g.base).homEquiv
511	                S.presheaf _) τ ≫
512	                (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map
513	                  ((TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ_fg') = (f ≫ g).c := by
514	            have h1 : ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat g.base).homEquiv
515	                S.presheaf _) τ =
516	                (TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).unit.app
517	                  S.presheaf := by
518	              dsimp only [τ]
519	              exact Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _
520	            rw [h1]
521	            -- adj_fg.unit.app S.presheaf ≫ pushforward g.map (pushforward f.map φ_fg')
522	            -- = adj_fg.unit.app S.presheaf ≫ pushforward (f≫g).map φ_fg'  [rfl]
523	            -- = adj_fg.homEquiv φ_fg' = (f ≫ g).c   [Equiv.apply_symm_apply on φ_fg']
524	            change (TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat
525	                (f ≫ g).base).homEquiv S.presheaf X.presheaf φ_fg' = _
526	            dsimp only [φ_fg']
527	            exact Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _
528	          have eq2 : ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat g.base).homEquiv
529	                S.presheaf Y.presheaf) φ_g' ≫
530	                (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map f.c = (f ≫ g).c := by
531	            have h4 : ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat g.base).homEquiv
532	                S.presheaf Y.presheaf) φ_g' = g.c := by
533	              dsimp only [φ_g']
534	              exact Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _
535	            rw [h4]
536	            rfl
537	          rw [eq1, eq2]
538	        -- Read off the coherence at U applied to a, getting the rewriting of
539	        -- (f.c.app U).hom (φ_g'.app U a) in terms of φ_fg'.
540	        change D_X.d ((ConcreteCategory.hom (f.c.app U))
541	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (φ_g'.app U)) a)) = 0
542	        have happ : (ConcreteCategory.hom (f.c.app U))
543	              ((ConcreteCategory.hom (φ_g'.app U)) a) =
544	            (ConcreteCategory.hom (φ_fg'.app
545	              (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))))
546	              ((ConcreteCategory.hom (τ.app U)) a) := by
547	          have h := congr_arg (fun h : _ ⟶ _ =>
548	            (ConcreteCategory.hom (NatTrans.app h U)) a) hτ.symm
549	          simpa using h
550	        rw [happ]
551	        exact D_X.d_app _ }
552	  let presheafHom : relativeDifferentialsPresheaf g ⟶
553	      (PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj
554	        (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g)) :=
555	    (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').desc d_target
556	  exact ⟨presheafHom⟩

559	-- GAP-FILL (iter-083, Lane 2): The η-coherence square underlying the descent
560	-- of `cotangentExactSeqBeta`. Fully closed via the inline adjunction chain
561	-- preserved verbatim from the iter-081 in-flight body. This is one of four
562	-- permitted helpers for `cotangentExactSeq_structure`. The lemma is in
563	-- existential form (∃ η, η ≫ φ_2' = φ_1') so that the witness η is
564	-- accessible by `obtain` inside both `cotangentExactSeqBeta`'s body and the
565	-- `h_epi` branch of `cotangentExactSeq_structure`.
566	/-- The η-coherence square underlying the descent of `cotangentExactSeqBeta`.
567	
568	For schemes `X →f Y →g S`, there exists a natural transformation of
569	ring-presheaves
570	`η : (g ∘ f)⁻¹ 𝒪_S ⟶ f_* (f⁻¹ 𝒪_Y)`
571	satisfying the coherence `η ≫ φ₂' = φ₁'`, where
572	`φ₁' = (adj_{(f≫g).base}.homEquiv).symm (f ≫ g).c` and
573	`φ₂' = (adj_{f.base}.homEquiv).symm f.c` are the adjoint transposes of
574	the comorphisms `(f ≫ g).c` and `f.c`. This coherence is what packages
575	the natural map into the descent map underlying `β`. -/
576	lemma cotangentExactSeqBeta_hη (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
577	    ∃ (η : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).obj S.presheaf ⟶
578	            (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj Y.presheaf),
579	        η ≫ ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv
580	              Y.presheaf X.presheaf).symm f.c =
581	          ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat
582	              (f ≫ g).base).homEquiv S.presheaf X.presheaf).symm (f ≫ g).c := by
583	  let φ1' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).homEquiv
584	    S.presheaf X.presheaf).symm (f ≫ g).c
585	  let φ2' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv
586	    Y.presheaf X.presheaf).symm f.c
587	  let adj_f := TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base
588	  let adj_fg := TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base
589	  let η : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).obj S.presheaf ⟶
590	      (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj Y.presheaf :=
591	    (adj_fg.homEquiv S.presheaf
592	      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj Y.presheaf)).symm
593	      (g.c ≫ (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map
594	        (adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf))
595	  refine ⟨η, ?_⟩
596	  change η ≫ φ2' = φ1'
597	  have h1 : adj_fg.homEquiv S.presheaf X.presheaf (η ≫ φ2') =
598	      adj_fg.homEquiv S.presheaf ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj Y.presheaf) η
599	        ≫ (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).map φ2' := by
600	    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right]
601	  have h2 : adj_fg.homEquiv S.presheaf
602	      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj Y.presheaf) η =
603	      g.c ≫ (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map
604	        (adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf) := by
605	    dsimp [η]
606	    exact Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _
607	  have h3 : (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).map φ2' =
608	      (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map
609	        ((TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ2') := rfl
610	  have h4 : adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫
611	      (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ2' = f.c := by
612	    have : adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫
613	        (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ2' =
614	        adj_f.homEquiv Y.presheaf X.presheaf φ2' := rfl
615	    rw [this]
616	    exact Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _
617	  have h5 : adj_fg.homEquiv S.presheaf X.presheaf (η ≫ φ2') = (f ≫ g).c := by
618	    rw [h1, h2, h3]
619	    have h6 : (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map
620	          (adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf) ≫
621	        (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map
622	          ((TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ2') =
623	        (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map f.c := by
624	      rw [← Functor.map_comp]
625	      congr 1
626	    calc
627	      (g.c ≫ (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map
628	            (adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf)) ≫
629	          (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map
630	            ((TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ2') =
631	        g.c ≫ ((TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map
632	            (adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf) ≫
633	          (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map
634	            ((TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ2')) := by
635	        simp only [Category.assoc]
636	      _ = g.c ≫ (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map f.c := by erw [h6]
637	      _ = (f ≫ g).c := rfl
638	  have h7 : adj_fg.homEquiv S.presheaf X.presheaf φ1' = (f ≫ g).c := by
639	    dsimp [φ1']
640	    exact Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _
641	  rw [← h7] at h5
642	  exact (adj_fg.homEquiv S.presheaf X.presheaf).injective h5
